$comment: Reference case for aces_tonemap (f(x) = (x*(a*x+b)) / (x*(c*x+d)+e)), the Narkowicz ACES filmic fit. Five coefficients a..e, but the rational form has a global scaling degeneracy — multiplying all five by any constant leaves f(x) unchanged — so the coefficients are individually non-identifiable. This case therefore asserts fit quality plus max_abs_error (the user-visible tonemap artefact is the worst-case error, not the mean), and omits per-coefficient bounds.
notes: 40-point sweep over HDR radiance x ∈ [0,8]. The curve runs from 0 through the mid-grey region to its ≈1.03 asymptote (a/c). max_abs_error_max=0.01 is the headline assertion — a tonemap regression usually shows up as a localised seam (good RMSE, bad worst-case) rather than a global RMSE drift. No coefficient bounds: the 5-parameter rational is scale-degenerate.
